name: investigating-codebases
description: Automatically activated when user asks how something works, wants to understand unfamiliar code, needs to explore a new codebase, or asks questions like "where is X implemented?", "how does Y work?", or "explain the Z component
capabilities: Map directory structures and identify architectural patterns
Trace import and dependency chains across files
Find main entry points across different project types
Recognize design patterns and coding conventions
Document execution flows with file references
Follow data transformations through the system
author: C0ntr0lledCha0s
securitySummary: This skill is a prompt-based investigation guide with 3 utility scripts for code analysis. All scripts only read files and output to stdout without network access, file writes, or credential handling. The static analyzer flagged 274 potential issues, but all are false positives caused by documentation examples and legitimate analysis terminology. No actual command execution, credential access, or network calls exist.
valueStatement: Understanding new or complex codebases takes significant time and effort. This skill provides systematic investigation techniques to quickly map code structure, trace dependencies, identify patterns, and find key implementations without reading every file manually.
limitations: Does not execute or test code - only analyzes static code
Cannot modify or refactor code automatically
Analysis is based on file reading - no runtime behavior observation
Relies on existing code structure - obfuscated code may limit effectiveness

useCases.title: Onboard to existing projects
useCases.description: Quickly understand unfamiliar codebases when joining a new team or project

useCases.title: Trace feature implementations
useCases.description: Find exactly where specific features or logic are implemented with full context

useCases.title: Map attack surfaces
useCases.description: Understand code organization to identify potential security vulnerabilities

promptTemplates.title: Basic feature exploration
promptTemplates.prompt: How is the [feature name] feature implemented? Trace the execution flow from the entry point to the core logic.

promptTemplates.title: Dependency analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: What modules does [file/module] depend on? Show me the import chain and how it connects to other parts of the system.

promptTemplates.title: Pattern identification
promptTemplates.prompt: What architectural pattern does this codebase follow? Identify the key directories and their purposes.

promptTemplates.title: Deep dive analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Do a thorough investigation of [component]. Include: entry points, execution flow, data transformations, dependencies, and related components. Reference all files with line numbers.

bestPractices: Start with high-level structure before diving into implementation details
Always reference specific files and line numbers in findings
Trace both execution flow and data transformations for complete understanding
antiPatterns: Reading every file linearly instead of targeting relevant code
Failing to verify findings by cross-referencing multiple sources
Not documenting where you found information as you investigate

faq.question: What programming languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill works with any language. Scripts specifically support TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Go, and Rust for import tracing and entry point detection.

faq.question: How deep does the dependency tracing go?
faq.answer: Default depth is 3 levels but can be configured. The Python scripts accept a --depth parameter to control how far they follow import chains.

faq.question: Can this skill integrate with other Claude skills?
faq.answer: Yes. The allowed tools include Task, so it can delegate to other agents for complex multi-step investigations.

faq.question: Is any code executed during analysis?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reads and analyzes code statically. It does not run or execute any code from the target codebase.

faq.question: Why are my findings different from expected?
faq.answer: Ensure you are looking at the correct files. Check package.json or project config to understand the actual entry points and structure before deep investigation.

faq.question: How is this different from simple grep searches?
faq.answer: This skill provides a systematic methodology with scripts for comprehensive analysis, not just keyword searches. It guides you through structural, dependency, and pattern analysis.
